About the Series

Built for operators
who are building.

Strong backend systems are what let mission-driven organizations grow without breaking. This series makes that practical.

What This Series Does

  • Demonstrate how systems support fundraising, events, partnerships, and team coordination
  • Create space for cross-functional expertise across operations, marketing, strategy, and infrastructure
  • Provide practical, real-world insights into operational systems and workflows
  • Equip leaders with frameworks they can immediately apply

Who This Is For

Nonprofit leaders and program managers
Founders and operators managing small or lean teams
Mission-driven organizations navigating growth and operational complexity
Individuals responsible for programs, events, partnerships, or fundraising
Session Topics

Four sessions.
One throughline.

Each session explores a different dimension of operational strength — from team structure to AI integration.

1
Session One
You Are Not the System
Building structure in small but mighty teams
2
Session Two
Systems That Fund
Building repeatable fundraising & partnerships
3
Session Three
From Manual to Managed
Automation, AI & reducing operational load
4
Session Four
Executing Without Chaos
Events, campaigns & minute coordination
Series Format

How it works.

4-part webinar series
45–60 minutes per session
Live, virtual format
Guided discussion + audience Q&A

Collaborator Information

What to expect
as a contributor.

🤝

Your Role

  • Participate in one guided session as a featured speaker or panelist
  • Share your expertise in a structured conversation
  • Be featured in all promotional materials for the series
📣

What You Provide

Headshot
Short bio (2–3 sentences)
Logo (if applicable)
Social handles / website

Assets used for all promotional materials.

✨

What You Get

  • Targeted visibility with nonprofit leaders & operators
  • Share expertise in a high-impact, curated setting
  • Expand your network through aligned partnerships
  • Access to recordings & promotional content
